soap:
REVIEW · lancedb/lancedb#2921 — feat: support add_columns with embedding function
zhangyue19921010 (first_time) · L (+259/-5, 5 files)  ⚠ escalation: public-api forced

ONE-LINER
  Adds a Python `Table.add_columns(EmbeddingFunctionConfig)` path that embeds a
  source column into a new fixed-size-list vector column via a Python UDF, and
  writes the embedding-function metadata into the table schema. Plumbed through
  a new pyo3 `add_columns_with_embedding_function` (Python-handler → Rust
  RecordBatch mapper → lance `NewColumnTransform::BatchUDF`) plus a new
  `update_schema_metadata` pyo3 method and a thin `add_columns_with_mapper`
  wrapper on the Rust `Table`.

SUBJECTIVE (intent)
  Useful, real feature: lets users add an embedding column after table creation
  while recording the embedding-function metadata so later `search(str)` works.
  Body of the PR in GitHub metadata was about an unrelated cache change at packet
  time, but the actual diff is squarely the embedding-column feature; reviewed
  against the diff, not the body.

OBJECTIVE (what I checked, tied to ground truth)
  • python/src/table.rs:802 `update_schema_metadata` — clones the manifest,
    swaps in a new `Schema { fields, metadata }`, and sets
    `dataset.manifest = Arc::new(manifest)` on a `get_mut()` guard. I traced the
    guard: rust/lancedb/src/table/dataset.rs:286 `DatasetWriteGuard` is a bare
    `RwLockWriteGuard` with only `Deref`/`DerefMut` (lines 290-307) and NO `Drop`
    impl. So this assignment mutates ONLY the in-process cached `Dataset`; it
    issues no commit and writes no new manifest to storage. The rest of the crate
    persists changes through commit operations (rust/lancedb/src/table.rs uses
    commit/Operation throughout), never by poking `dataset.manifest`. => the
    embedding-function metadata is NOT durable. (BLOCKING)
  • Ordering compounds it: python/python/lancedb/table.py:4271 dispatch runs
    `update_embedding_function_metadata` (the in-memory poke) FIRST, then
    `add_columns_with_embedding_function` which performs a real
    `NewColumnTransform::BatchUDF` commit. That commit advances the table version
    and reloads the manifest from storage, discarding the in-memory metadata
    poke. So after `add_columns` returns, `schema.metadata` very likely has NO
    embedding config. (BLOCKING — same root cause)
  • python/python/tests/test_table.py:1681 — the only test for the feature is
    `@pytest.mark.slow` and `pytest.importorskip("sentence_transformers")`. The
    passing CI matrix (Linux py3.9/3.12, Mac, Windows, Type Check) does NOT run
    `slow` tests, and sentence-transformers is unlikely installed there, so the
    feature's happy path is effectively UNVERIFIED by CI. The assertion
    `parsed["vector"].source_column == "text"` (line 1710) is exactly the durability
    claim that the manifest analysis says should fail on a fresh read — strong
    signal the test was only ever run, if at all, against in-process state.
    (NOT covered by CI)
  • python/src/table.rs:751 + :802 — both new fns are inside the
    `#[pymethods] impl Table` block (267-909), so they ARE registered/callable
    from Python. Verified, not an issue.
  • Schema field-name match: Python handler builds `pa.list_(float32, ndims)`
    whose inner field is `item`/nullable; Rust output_schema declares inner
    `Field::new("item", Float32, true)` (python/src/table.rs ~:770). Names and
    nullability match, so lance's BatchUDF output validation should accept the
    mapper output. Verified (could not run pyarrow locally to double-check the
    default inner-field name, but it is `item` by pyarrow convention). 
  • python/src/table.rs ~:786 mapper error handling collapses any Python
    exception from the embedding handler into a generic
    `InvalidInput { source: "computing embedding failed" }`, dropping the original
    Python traceback. Debuggability nit, not gating. (non-blocking)
  • python/python/lancedb/table.py:4298 `update_embedding_function_metadata`
    raises if a function already exists for the vector column ("Modifying ...
    not supported") — reasonable guard. But because the metadata write is not
    durable (above), this guard also can't actually see previously-persisted
    functions across sessions. Resolves once persistence is fixed.

ASSESSMENT
  risk: HIGH (refined up from baseline med) — IO/format-correctness: a new
    public write path that claims to persist schema metadata but does not commit
    it, so it silently loses data the user believes is stored. This is exactly the
    class the baseline can't see from paths alone.
  urgency: high (baseline) — feature PR, first-time contributor.
  escalation: forced on `public-api` → full rounds regardless. New public Python
    surface (`add_columns(EmbeddingFunctionConfig)`) + two new pyo3 methods.
  merge_state: GitHub reports mergeable_state="dirty" (merge conflict vs main)
    and not mergeable — must rebase. CI is effectively green (only `pydantic1x`
    cancelled, not failed); the packet's ci="red" is stale/conservative.
  could not read: none (0 omitted; all 5 files read).

PLAN / DRAFT COMMENTS
  issue(blocking): Schema-metadata write is not durable.
    `update_schema_metadata` mutates the in-memory `dataset.manifest` on a
    get_mut guard with no commit, so the embedding-function metadata is never
    written to storage and is discarded by the subsequent BatchUDF commit.
    acceptance: metadata persists across a reopen — i.e. perform the schema-
    metadata update via a real lance commit (replace_schema_metadata / a manifest
    commit operation), AND add a non-slow test that reopens the table (new
    connection/handle) and asserts `parse_functions(schema.metadata)["vector"]`
    is present. (python/src/table.rs:802, table.py:4271)

  issue(blocking): Feature happy-path has no CI-exercised test.
    The only test is `slow` + importorskip(sentence_transformers), so it does not
    run in the passing matrix.
    acceptance: add a test using a trivial in-repo/stub embedding function (no
    sentence-transformers dependency) that runs in the default CI matrix and
    asserts (a) the vector column is added with correct dtype/ndims and (b) the
    embedding metadata is readable after reopen. (test_table.py:1681)

  issue(non-blocking): Mapper swallows the Python embedding error.
    Collapsing to "computing embedding failed" drops the original traceback;
    propagate the Python error message into the InvalidInput source.
    (python/src/table.rs ~:786)

  suggestion: Consider writing the metadata as part of the same transaction/
    commit as the column add (or after it) rather than a separate pre-step, so a
    failure in the embedding step can't leave a half-applied state and so a single
    version captures both. (table.py:4271)

  question: Is `add_columns_with_embedding_function` intended to also be exposed
    on the sync `Table` and on remote tables? `update_schema_metadata` errors on
    remote ("This cannot be run on a remote table"); is remote explicitly out of
    scope for this PR? (python/src/table.rs:802)

RECOMMENDATION: block on 2 conditions (durable metadata write + CI-exercised
  test). Plus rebase to clear the merge conflict before merge.
  confidence: high on the durability bug — traced the guard to no-Drop/no-commit
    and confirmed the crate commits everything else; the test asserts exactly the
    property that should fail on reopen.
  what would make this wrong: only if a raw `dataset.manifest = ...` assignment
    were itself durable. It is not: a trace of the lance commit path confirms the
    durable route is `Dataset::replace_schema_metadata` →
    `Operation::UpdateConfig` → `apply_commit` (writes the manifest to object
    storage, THEN updates the in-memory `dataset.manifest`). The PR skips that
    entire path and assigns the in-memory field directly, so nothing is written.
    This corroborates the block and names the fix: use `replace_schema_metadata`.
